name: Viridis
description: Perceptually uniform sequential colormap transitioning dark purple to bright yellow
notes: Default choice for most scientific visualization; remains readable when converted to grayscale

name: Magma
description: Perceptually uniform sequential colormap from black through deep purple to cream-white
notes: Elegant dark aesthetic; excellent for high-dynamic-range audio

name: Inferno
description: Perceptually uniform sequential colormap from black through red/orange to yellow-white
notes: Visually striking 'fire' aesthetic; similar to Magma but warmer mid-range

name: Plasma
description: Perceptually uniform sequential colormap from deep blue-purple through pink to yellow
notes: High-saturation alternative to Viridis; very eye-catching for presentations

name: Hot
description: Classic sequential colormap: black → red → orange → yellow → white
notes: Classic 'thermal' look; not perceptually uniform but very familiar in engineering contexts

name: Cool
description: Diverging-like sequential colormap from cyan to magenta
best_for: Acoustic/water wave spectrograms, sonar, underwater cymatics
notes: Aquatic/cool aesthetic; good for water cymatics visualizations and sonar data

name: Jet
description: Legacy rainbow colormap: blue → cyan → green → yellow → red
notes: NOT recommended for publication. Creates false perceptual boundaries; problematic for colorblind viewers. Use Viridis instead.

name: Grayscale
best_for: Print publications, MRI/CT scans, accessible visualizations
notes: Fully accessible; converts perfectly to print. Often used inverted (white background, dark signal) for publications.

name: Twilight
description: Perceptually uniform cyclic colormap from dark through light and back to dark; good for phase data
best_for: Phase spectrograms, cyclic data, polar plots
notes: Cyclic colormap — wraps around. Ideal for phase angle visualization where 0° and 360° should have the same color.

name: Cymatics
description: Custom colormap inspired by cymatics water patterns: deep ocean purple → royal blue → cyan → bright white-cyan
best_for: Cymatics visualizations, water/fluid dynamics spectrograms, resonance patterns
notes: Designed to evoke the aesthetic of cymatics water patterns. Dark void at zero, building through ocean depths to bright energetic white-cyan peaks.
